name: The Transformation Letter — Brand Style v3 (Wadi Bardawil Brand System)
voice: advisor not pundit, diagnostic not promotional, restrained not loud

paletteNotes.background: gunmetal — page background, header, primary dark surface
paletteNotes.primary: same as background — gunmetal, never replaced with another color
paletteNotes.surface: panel — elevated card / surface, slightly lifted off background
paletteNotes.foreground: primary text on dark — pure white
paletteNotes.mutedForeground: secondary text, captions, deprioritized content (body text uses #B8C0CC)
paletteNotes.accent: brand orange — CTAs and highlights ONLY, ≤10% of any surface. Never decorative.

typography.display: Archivo, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif
typography.body: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif
typography.mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace

scale.tracking: tight (-0.015em on headings); eyebrows uppercase with wide tracking

imageStyle.approach: sophisticated editorial illustration inspired by The New York Times, The Economist, Financial Times; intellectual and minimalist approach, strategic storytelling through visuals; professional illustration feeling.
constraints: no realistic photography of identifiable people unless cleared
no stock-photo executives in suits or boardrooms
no AI tropes (robot hands, glowing brains, circuit boards, futuristic UI panels)
no clichéd transformation imagery (caterpillar/butterfly, sunrise/sunset, ladder, mountaintop, lightbulb)
no comic style, no cartoon style, no exaggerated characters
no overloaded compositions, no multiple simultaneous messages, no excessive decorative elements
no flags, logos, brand marks, or trademarked imagery (except for a small, discreet WB logo in a corner, only once, never competing with the main message)
no decorative gradients — flat composition only
no light backgrounds — imagery must read on the dark gunmetal canvas
no bright primary colors (other than specified orange accent)

no text or words in the image unless explicitly requested as an editorial headline, strategic statement, or memorable insight (max 8-12 words, editorial serif typography, similar to premium newspapers)
preferences: elegant pencil sketch style, hand-drawn graphite lines, handmade texture, refined details
abstract geometric forms suggesting structure, sequence, and process
architectural metaphors: foundations, scaffolding, blueprints, load-bearing walls
limited palette: gunmetal charcoals (#222831 family) + white + brand orange #FD4002 highlight (used sparingly, only when the subject demands it)
negative space as a deliberate composition element
matte finish; no shiny gradients, no neon, no chrome
white backgrounds, off-white backgrounds, clean spaces, large areas of negative space
environments representing modern offices, technology environments, contemporary corporate scenarios, strategic business contexts
one central idea, a clear visual metaphor, defined visual hierarchy, minimal but meaningful elements

captionVoice.guidance: One sentence. Anchors the image to the issue's diagnostic — not a description of the picture. Same voice as the letter: advisor, present tense, second-person where natural.
examplesOk: The structure your team needs is not a chart — it is the artifact you have not written yet.
Decision rights are scaffolding. Without them, every floor of the business rests on you.
examplesAvoid: An abstract illustration of business transformation.
Photo of business strategy and AI working together.

publisher.name: Wadi Bardawil
publisher.positioningTagline: I build the intelligence layer between your decisions and your operations.
publisher.location: Monterrey, Mexico — serving Mexico & United States
